abstract: This article is dedicated to Prof. George Lotter, a pivotal figure in the development of more than 90 postgraduate students in Practical Theology at North-West University (NWU). Under his mentorship, there has been a significant surge in empirical research, reflecting a broader global shift toward evidence-based inquiry within the discipline. As Practical Theology increasingly engages with complex societal issues, the development of robust empirical research capacities among both lecturers and students has become essential. This article identifies a specific need for more rigorous methodology during the qualitative analysis phase of research. By evaluating current research outputs at NWU and synthesizing established qualitative traditions, the author argues for a more systematic approach to data coding. The article culminates in a proposed five-phase model designed to enhance the credibility and depth of data analysis in practical theological studies.
